habitat: Forests; woodland; cultivated areas; and open country
story: In the diverse world of birds, the Black Baza (Aviceda leuphotes) stands out. This species, part of the Accipitridae family, is a resident of Forests; woodland; cultivated areas; and open country. Its IUCN Red List status is Least Concern (LC), which serves as a call to action for habitat preservation. The Black Baza is more than just a bird; it is a symbol of the rich biodiversity that we must protect. Through this poster, we hope to foster a greater understanding and appreciation for the Black Baza and its role in the ecosystem.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
